title: linaria repens var. monspessulana
intro: Linaria repens var. monspessulana, also known as the sticky or selfheal, is a plant species within the figwort family that has been used for medicinal and culinary purposes for centuries. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Linaria repens var. monspessulana include teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
